steps.title: Track the logical setup
steps.content: Mooney sorted Cherokee stories into genres — including a "wonder stories" genre defined by detailed geographic descriptions. The final sentence flags that Cherokee people didn't apply genre divisions to their stories before Mooney did. The blank follows "There is, therefore," so it must state a conclusion that follows from that fact.

steps.title: Match the inference
steps.content:
If the Cherokee elders didn't use genre divisions at all, there's no reason to assume they'd agree that geographical specificity set certain stories apart as a different kind. That's exactly choice D.

A reverses the logic (talks about stories not shared). B is about belief in geographic accuracy, not genre kinds. C shifts focus to Long's influence, which the passage doesn't address.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
